title: The Traveler
subtitle: Ways & Journeys

lore.description: The Traveler is the eternal guide of the road, guardian of those who walk the paths between what is and what might be. Among the Fhara caravans and Kuvani traders along the Way of Silk, the Traveler is invoked at every crossroads, honored with small offerings at each waypoint, and consulted before every major journey.
lore.followers: The Traveler is not merely one who shows the way — they are the way, existing in the pause between steps and in the choice of which path to take when roads fork. Every journey is both physical and spiritual; to move from one place to another is to transform, and the road itself becomes a teacher.

names_across_regions.kahfagia: The Lantern-Bearer, whose light guides ships through uncharted waters
names_across_regions.ykrul: The Hoof-Print, the spirit of the steppe road that appears as a single arrowhead left at a crossroads
names_across_regions.aeler: The Ways-Stone, carved into every mile marker by True-Masons to ensure the road remembers those who walk it
names_across_regions.mistlands: Invoked with a single unknotted bell-rope — a sign that the fog may part for one who walks humbly

lore.quote: One foot in a promise, and the road will meet you halfway. But break your word to the way, and the way will break you.

domain_focus: Thresholds of Movement: doors, bridges, crossroads, borders
Road Covenants: promises made on the road, hospitality to travelers

thiasos.description: A Runekeeper sworn to the Traveler does not bind a familiar of hearth and home — they bind a companion of the open road. Their Thiasos is a small beast or mythological being that embodies movement and guidance: a gray hare that never tires, a sparrow that knows every shortcut, a small fox that leaves no tracks, a dung beetle that rolls its ball unerringly toward the nearest water, or a tiny brass compass that hums when the bearer is on the true path.
thiasos.care: This creature requires no food, but it must walk at least a mile each day (or be allowed to roam freely in a new place). If confined for more than a day, it becomes Compromised (it grows lethargic and its guidance becomes unreliable, imposing –1 die on all Navigation and Lore rolls for its bearer until it is exercised).

codex.description: The Codex of the Traveler is never a stationary book. It may be a leather strap knotted with directions, a set of scrolls kept in different saddlebags, a map that redraws itself after each journey, or a pilgrim's diary whose blank pages fill with route descriptions as the bearer walks.
codex.upkeep: Because the road resists being captured in ink, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week walking a new path and annotating it, or the Rites stored there become faded (treat as Compromised until updated).

patrons_gift.name: Traveler's Blessing
patrons_gift.description: Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Traveler may touch a held item (weapon, tool, or cloak) and whisper a waymark.
patrons_gift.effect: For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Navigation or Lore rolls, and the first time each scene the bearer would be slowed by difficult terrain or a bureaucratic delay, they may ignore it (the road opens for them).
patrons_gift.cost: After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Traveler.

rites.name: Rite of Road-Sense
rites.effect: Unerringly pick the fastest safe route in Near/Far. Gain +1 die to avoid ambushes or delays. Create a 4-segment Path Memory timer to ignore difficult terrain once.
rites.push_it: Spot one hidden bypass, but generate 1 SB (Clubs). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +1) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Traveler's Boon
rites.effect: Ignore one level of difficult terrain or bureaucratic delay; +1 Effect to travel or escape checks. If shared, create a 2-segment Shared Journey bond with an ally.
rites.push_it: Extend to one more ally; mark 1 SB (Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +1) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Waymark
rites.materials: A chalk mark or small cairn.
rites.effect: Declare a lane as permitted or easy. Allies gain improved Position/Effect or ignore one obstacle. Create a 6-segment Marked Path timer.
rites.push_it: The lane persists between scenes; the first enemy to exploit it forces 1 SB (Spades). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Bridge Between
rites.effect: Relocate a willing target within Far along a visible or named route. Unwilling targets may resist (Body+Resolve DV 3). Create a 4-segment Pathway Established timer.
rites.push_it: Carry one extra ally or bundle; arrivals are off-balance. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Crown of Crossings
rites.materials: A brass compass missing its needle.
rites.effect: Call the Road: allies gain +1 die to move or evade; pursuers suffer –1 die. Once, declare 'the long way is short' to finish a travel timer segment for free. Enemies must test (Wits+Command DV 4) or suffer –1 die to movement.
rites.push_it: Seal a hostile route briefly; generate 2 SB (Clubs/Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Wayfarer's Covenant
rites.effect: Bind present parties to safe passage. While honored: +2 Effect on travel, reroll one failed travel roll per scene. Breaking the oath inflicts Harm 1 (Fatigue) and marks the breaker as Oathbreaker of the Road (–2 dice to travel rolls).
rites.push_it: Breaking the covenant inflicts Harm 2 (Stress) and attracts hostile Wayward spirits. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

cantors.name: The Cantor's Song
cantors.description: A Cantor of the Traveler sings in verses of the open road — melodies that rise and fall like hills, harmonies that echo the creak of wagon wheels. Their voice is used to mark a crossroads with a sung sign, to calm a spooked pack animal, or to call a lost wanderer back to the path. The Traveler's Cantors are often found in caravan camps, at pilgrimage shrines, and walking the roads alone, humming to keep the miles company.

cult.name: The Cult of the Unbroken Way
cult.description: The Traveler's cults gather at forks, at bridges, and at the crests of passes — never in buildings. The Unbroken Way is a fellowship of guides, couriers, scouts, and those who have walked so far that the road has become their home. A ritual of the Cult involves each member placing a small stone at a crossroads, then walking a different way home than they came — the road must be honored with new steps. Their creed is scratched into a single waystone that travels from meeting to meeting: 'The road does not end. It only turns.' Outsiders are welcome to walk a mile with the cult, but they must carry a stone from home and leave it at the next crossroads.

witchcraft.description: A witch who walks with the Traveler is a map-scribe or waystone-carver — one who crafts the physical markers of the road: a compass that always points to a chosen destination, a map that shows routes that appear only when needed, or a staff that never causes the bearer to trip. Their magic works through the materials of travel: a quill that draws paths that become real by morning, a chisel that carves a symbol into a stone so that travelers who touch it feel rested, or a needle that sews a patch into a cloak that keeps the wind from shifting direction.

way_chisel.name: The Way Chisel
way_chisel.description: A chisel of iron, its head struck with a simple arrow. Once per session, the witch may carve a 'way mark' into a stone, a tree, or a wooden post. For the next day, anyone who sees the way mark gains a perfect sense of direction to the nearest safe harbor, village, or crossroads — but only while walking. The chisel can also be used to 'erase' a false trail, removing tracks from a path as if they had never been made (one exchange of effort per use).

hedge_gifts.name: Road-Worn Token
hedge_gifts.description: Once per scene, the witch may touch a small stone, a coin, or a button that has been carried on a long journey. For the next hour, the token glows faintly when the bearer is on the correct path to their goal (as long as the goal is a physical location). The token does not reveal the path — only that the bearer has not (or has) strayed from it.

corruption.benefit: Pathfinding Instinct: +1 die to Navigation and movement rolls in familiar territory.
corruption.cost: Restless Spirit: You feel the urge to keep moving. Staying in one place for too long makes you slightly uncomfortable, but this imposes no mechanical penalty.

corruption.benefit: Wayfarer's Luck: Once/scene, treat a failed travel or navigation roll as a success, but mark 1 SB (Clubs).
corruption.cost: Wandering Streak: You are drawn to the less obvious path. Following a direct route is possible, but requires a moment's thought.

corruption.benefit: Road Mastery: +2 dice to rolls involving travel, escape, or finding hidden paths.
corruption.cost: Nomadic Habits: –1 die to settlement-based social rolls until you have spent at least a day in a place.

corruption.benefit: Journey's Blessing: Once/session, grant all allies within Near range +1 die to travel-related rolls for one scene.
corruption.cost: Road's Call: You feel a strong pull toward the horizon; being cooped up is genuinely unpleasant but not debilitating.

corruption.benefit: Waypoint Sense: Once/session, instantly know the location and nature of all significant paths, routes, or crossroads within a day's travel.
corruption.cost: Path Obsession: You tend to think in terms of routes and destinations, sometimes missing the point of staying still.

corruption.benefit: Master of All Ways: Once/session, become one with the road network. For one scene, teleport between any known waypoints and grant +2 dice to all travel-related actions.
corruption.cost: Eternal Journey: Mark +3 Obligation when using this power. The road is your home now; you can still function in settlements, but you will always be looking for the way out.

playstyle_notes.description: The Traveler is the ideal patron for beginners and veterans alike — its rites are forgiving, its Obligation low, and its benefits immediately useful in almost any campaign. Followers become masters of navigation, escape, and the subtle art of being in the right place at the right time. The Traveler does not demand worship; it asks only that travelers respect the road and keep their bargains. Intrusions often take the form of a sudden fork in the path, a helpful stranger who knows too much, or a road that leads somewhere unexpected. Ideal for guides, couriers, scouts, pilgrims, and anyone who has ever felt the pull of the horizon.
emphasizes: Wayfinding: the art of never being lost
Road Covenants: a promise made on the road is binding
Journey as Transformation: the road changes the walker
Thresholds of Movement: doors, bridges, borders, and the spaces between
Hospitality to Travelers: the road gives, and the road expects

monastic_tradition.name: Way-Walker (The Traveler)
monastic_tradition.quote: One foot in a promise, and the road will meet you halfway.
monastic_tradition.prerequisites: Wits 3+, Spirit 2+, Rite of Road-Sense or Cantor of The Traveler.
monastic_tradition.debt_resistant_frame: The road does not hold grudges; it simply turns. Monks of the Way-Walker tradition gain +1 die to resist any magical debt or obligation that would bind them to a single place or force them to stay still. If they succeed, the creditor's chains find only empty air — the monk is already a mile down the road.

techniques.name: Way-Step
techniques.effect: Teleport to any unoccupied space you can see within Near range. You must have line of sight and an unobstructed path (the road folds, not breaks).

techniques.name: Road-Sense
techniques.effect: Unerringly pick the fastest safe route in Near/Far. Gain +1 die to avoid ambushes or delays. Create a 4-segment Path Memory timer.

techniques.name: Traveler's Boon
techniques.effect: Ignore one level of difficult terrain or bureaucratic delay. +1 Effect to travel or escape checks.

techniques.name: Waymark
techniques.effect: Declare a lane as permitted or easy. Allies gain improved Position/Effect or ignore one obstacle. Create a 6-segment Marked Path timer.

techniques.name: Bridge Between
techniques.effect: Relocate a willing target within Far along a visible or named route. Unwilling targets resist with Body+Resolve (DV 3).

techniques.name: Crown of Crossings
techniques.effect: For one scene, allies gain +1 die to move or evade; pursuers suffer –1 die. Once, declare 'the long way is short' to finish a travel timer segment for free.

master_technique.name: Master of All Ways
master_technique.description: Once per session, become one with the road network for one scene. Teleport between any two known waypoints (places you have visited and marked with a personal token — a pebble, a scratch on a door) within the same journey. You may also grant one ally the same teleport. After the scene, you gain a permanent Debt Scar (your footprints face the wrong direction) — but you may ignore one debt or obligation that would force you to stay; the road simply does not remember you were ever there.

sample_adventure.title: The Road That Forgot
sample_adventure.description: A stretch of the King's High Road has begun to forget itself. Travelers report walking for days and arriving back where they started, or finding bridges that lead to empty air. The local waystone-carvers are baffled; the road's memory is fading. The party must walk the road, find the source of the forgetting — a forgotten oath, a broken waystone, or something older — and restore the path before the road forgets itself entirely. The Traveler's servants are divided: some believe the road must be re-consecrated; others believe it must be allowed to become something new.
sample_adventure.quote: A road that forgets itself is a road that has been betrayed. Find the broken promise, mend it, and the way will remember. But some roads are better left forgotten — and the Traveler does not always answer the ones who call.
